paper_metadata.title: A Generalist Agent
paper_metadata.abstract: Inspired by progress in large-scale language modeling, we apply a similar approach towards building a single generalist agent beyond the realm of text outputs. The agent, which we refer to as Gato, works as a multi-modal, multi-task, multi-embodiment generalist policy. The same network with the same weights can play Atari, caption images, chat, stack blocks with a real robot arm and much more, deciding based on its context whether to output text, joint torques, button presses, or other tokens. In this report we describe the model and the data, and document the current capabilities of Gato.

extraction_data.innovation: Gato is a generalist agent that uses a single transformer model with the same weights to perform multiple tasks across different modalities and embodiments. It serializes diverse data types into a flat sequence of tokens and trains as a large language model, enabling zero-shot transfer to new tasks through a common token-based representation.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: DMLab, ALE Atari, ALE Atari Extended, Sokoban, BabyAI, DM Control Suite, DM Control Suite Pixels, DM Control Suite Random Small, DM Control Suite Random Large, Meta-World, Procgen Benchmark, RGB Stacking simulator, RGB Stacking real robot, Modular RL, DM Manipulation Playground, MassiveText, M3W, ALIGN, MS-COCO Captions, Conceptual Captions, LTIP, OKVQA, VQAV2
extraction_data.extension: Uses learnable position encodings for both text and image tokens, and applies prompt conditioning during training and evaluation to disambiguate tasks.
extraction_data.research_problem: Building a generalist agent capable of multi-modal, multi-task, and multi-embodiment control using a single neural network
extraction_data.application: Playing Atari, image captioning, chat, robot arm manipulation, 3D navigation, instruction following
